title: A New Sensitive Method for Determination of 4-Nitrophenylhydrazine using a New Reagent System
author: PRATIMA VERMA
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Ravishankar University, Raipur-492 010 

  Manuscript received 3 February 1984, revised 29 July 2986, accepted 12 August 1986  

 A new sensitive method for the determination of 4-nitrophenylbydrazine has been proposed. The method is based on the oxidation of 4-nitrophenylhydrazine to 4-nitro­phenyldiazonium chloride with selenious acid in strong HCl medium. The diazonium salt is then coupled with  N -(1-naphthyDethylenediamine (NEDA) in HCl medium. Violet dye formed is measured at 545 nm against reagent blank. The reaction is fast, sensitive, and reproducible. It is simple and does not require rigid control of condi­tions. Acidity, temperature, time and reagent concentrations have been studied for the full colour development. Various analytical parameters, such as Beer's law, molar absorptivity, etc., have been evaluated.
